title: i2e Intermediate Representation (IR) v1
description: Load-bearing wall of the flywheel. Node ② (de-layer -> IR) WRITES this; the editor RENDERS it; Node ⑤ (correction capture) appends to `corrections`. Designed model-agnostic, format-neutral, serializable, third-party readable, and versioned. v1 scope: flat marketing images; edits = copy / localization / resize. Forward-compat: every object permits an `ext` bag for vendor extensions; unknown top-level fields are rejected to keep the standard tight.

ir_version.description: Schema version. Bump on breaking change; migrators key off this.

id.description: Document id (== image_id used by corrections).

brand_ref.description: Id of the brand profile applied (Node ⑦ brand memory). Null until a brand is bound.

elements.description: Scene-graph elements, ordered bottom -> top (index 0 = backmost). Explicit `z` is authoritative; array order is a convenience mirror.

corrections.description: Node ⑤ moat data. Append-only log of (predicted -> corrected) pairs produced as a side effect of the user editing to ship. Each entry is a labeled training example tagged with the sub-model that produced the prediction.

ext.description: Vendor/extension bag. Never read by the core spec.

generator.description: Best-effort origin of the flat image. Open vocabulary; common values below.

original_image_ref.description: URI/path to the original flattened raster (immutable; ground truth for the verification loop).

unit.description: v1 is pixel-space; physical units deferred.

background_element_id.description: Element id of the reconstructed clean background (Node ④ output of inpaint).

NBox.description: Normalized box (fractions of canvas). Carried alongside BBox so resize/reflow has a basis without a full constraint solver in v1.

ExtractionMeta.description: Provenance from Node ②. Confidence drives `needs_review` and the flywheel north-star metric (corrections per image).

model_version.description: REQUIRED so every later correction can be attributed to the exact model that erred.

ElementBase.description: Common fields on every element. Concrete element types add exactly one payload object matching `type`.

needs_review.description: Derived: extraction.confidence < threshold. The editor surfaces these first (HITL).

editable.description: Field paths the v1 editor exposes for this element (e.g. ['text.content','text.color']). Keeps the UI honest about what is safely editable.

content.description: Recognized text (OCR). The primary v1 editable field (copy edit + localization).

font_family.description: Recognized font; matched to a real family when possible (else best guess).

baked_region_ref.description: URI/path to the original raster crop where this text sat. Required input for inpainting the background behind it.

font_file.description: Resolved local font file path for rendering (set by font matching).

RasterPayload.description: A photographic foreground kept as pixels (e.g. the product shot). Not re-synthesizable in v1.

asset_ref.description: URI/path to the RGBA cutout PNG.

BackgroundPayload.description: Reconstructed clean background (Node ④). The biggest lossy point; its confidence is tracked separately so the flywheel can target it.

asset_ref.description: URI/path to the inpainted background raster.

LogoPayload.description: Brand mark. Prefer a matched real asset from the brand library (clean vector); fall back to traced vector, then raster.

matched_asset_id.description: Id in the brand asset library if recognized (Node ⑦). Best case: swap in the real vector.

VectorPayload.description: Icons, dividers, badges, simple shapes recovered as vectors.

svg_path.description: Inline SVG path data, for small shapes.

children.description: Ids of member elements (e.g. an icon + its label, or a co-brand lockup).

Correction.description: Node ⑤ unit of moat data. ONE field changed from the model's prediction to the user's shipped value. The genius: the user makes these to ship, not to label; we capture them for free.

image_id.description: == document id, so corrections join back to the source image.

element_id.description: Target element; null for document-level ops (e.g. element_add).

field_path.description: Dotted path of the corrected field, e.g. 'text.content', 'text.font_family', 'bbox', 'type', 'z'. Routes the training pair to the right sub-model.

kind.description: Correction category -> which extraction sub-model to retrain.

predicted.description: What Node ② produced. Any JSON value.

corrected.description: What the user finalized. Any JSON value.

confidence_at_prediction.description: Lets us check calibration: were low-confidence items the ones actually corrected?

model_version.description: Which model erred. Required for attributing the training signal.

user_hash.description: Pseudonymous user id for dedup/abuse, never PII.
